articles.title: USD/CAD Price Forecast: Gains ground above 1.3600, while bearish technical bias persists
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair posts modest gains near 1.3635 during the early European trading hours on Tuesday. Uncertainty over US-Iran peace talks and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz provide some support to a safe-haven currency such as the US Dollar (USD) against the Canadian Dollar (CAD).

articles.title: NZD/USD slips to 0.5900 as US-Iran tensions support USD; focus remains on FOMC meeting
articles.expand: The NZD/USD pair struggles to capitalize on its strong gains registered over the past two days and attracts some sellers during the Asian session on Tuesday.

articles.title: BoJ Hawkish Hold: 6–3 Split and Inflation Upgrade Point to Rate Hike Ahead
articles.expand: The Bank of Japan decided, as widely expected, to keep its short-term policy rate unchanged at 0.75%. While the “hold” was the majority decision, the meeting revealed a significant hawkish shift in the internal vote and a sharp revision to the bank’s economic projections. articles.title: USD/CHF gathers strength above 0.7850 as Fed rate decision looms
articles.expand: The USD/CHF pair gathers strength near 0.7865 during the early European session on Tuesday. The US Dollar (USD) edges higher against the Swiss Franc (CHF) due to the stalled US-Iran ceasefire and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: EUR/USD Price Forecast: Holds above 1.1700 as USD bulls seem hesitant ahead of FOMC meeting
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair trades with a mild negative bias during the Asian session on Tuesday and looks to extend the previous day's retracement slide from levels just above mid-1.1700s.

articles.title: Chart Art: EUR/JPY Major Pullback Ahead of ECB Decision

articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) on Monday fell by -0.05%. articles.title: Gold struggles below $4,700 as US-Iran tensions support USD ahead of FOMC meeting
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) trades with a negative bias below the $4,700 mark for the second consecutive day and slides back closer to last week's swing low during the Asian session on Tuesday.

articles.title: GBP/JPY slides to 215.25 after BoJ's hawkish pause; downside seems limited
articles.expand: The GBP/JPY cross attracts some sellers during the Asian session on Tuesday and, for now, seems to have snapped a two-day winning streak to its highest level since January 2008, just above the 216.00 mark touched the previous day.

articles.title: Bank of Japan holds interest rate at 0.75%, upgrades inflation forecasts
articles.expand: The Bank of Japan (BoJ) board members decided to leave the short-term interest rate unadjusted at 0.75%, following the conclusion of its two-day monetary policy review meeting on Tuesday.

articles.expand: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) oil price extends its gains for the second successive day, trading around $95.20 per barrel during the Asian hours on Tuesday. Crude oil prices rise as the critical Strait of Hormuz remains largely shut, tightening Middle East energy supplies.

articles.title: Pound Sterling softens as markets await Fed and BoE rate decisions
